pir: Will the tracked competitor make a material pricing change within 30 days, and if so in which direction and on which product line?

ground_truth.state: Cirrus is running a deliberate feint: it planted convincing Starter price-cut rumors in the trade press while internally approving a Pro-tier list-price increase from $99 to $129 effective next month. The sales organization has been briefed on the higher Pro pricing, a staged pricing page reflects the uplift, and the visible cut story is uncorroborated planted noise. The material action in the window is a genuine price increase on Pro, not the feint cut story.

signal_stream.content: A SaaS-pricing trade blog claims, citing unnamed sources, that Cirrus will cut Starter pricing within weeks.
An industry newsletter repeated the trade-blog rumor that Cirrus will cut Starter pricing, citing the original blog post.
A reliable contact inside Cirrus sales reports the team was briefed this week on new Pro-tier list pricing roughly 30 percent higher, effective at the start of next month.
A cached Cirrus pricing page draft shows the Pro plan at $129 per month instead of the current $99. The page is staged and not yet linked from the live site.
On a recent podcast the Cirrus CFO said the company is raising prices on the Pro tier to reflect enterprise-grade value and that a list-price uplift is imminent.
Cirrus announced a routine security patch for its mobile app.
